exemplar.title: Are some types of knowledge more useful than others?

detailedExemplarData.title: Are some types of knowledge more useful than others?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific real-world objects with contextual details
Well-articulated links between each object and the prompt about useful knowledge
Justification of each objectâs contribution grounded in TOK concepts (perspective, power)
Evidence of research through a works cited list, demonstrating engagement with external sources
weaknesses: Explanations are uneven in depth and sometimes framed as rhetorical questions rather than sustained analysis
Repetition of the same argument across objects limits originality of insights
Limited use of explicit TOK terminology and academic sources to support key claims
Conclusion restates general ideas without synthesizing how each object individually addressed the prompt
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition clearly meets the descriptors of band 7â8 by identifying three distinct objects in precise real-world contexts and providing strong justifications that tie each object back to the IA prompt. Most points are supported by appropriate evidence, and TOK concepts (e.g., gatekeepers, perspective) are invoked to show how decisions about âuseful knowledgeâ are made. However, the reasoning remains uneven: analysis often relies on rhetorical questions, arguments are repeated across objects, and explicit references to TOK terminology or scholarly sources are limited. These lapses in depth and precision prevent placement in the top band (9â10), but the overall coherence and strength of justification merit a solid score of 7.

annotations.comment: The introduction question effectively frames the exhibition but could benefit from a clearer operational definition of âusefulâ to set analytic boundaries and focus.
The student provides a solid definition of perspective, linking it to culture and beliefs. To strengthen, integrate immediately how this influences judgments about which knowledge is deemed useful.
Object 1 label is clear but the historical context could be more precise. Include commissioning year (1611) and translator backgrounds to anchor its real-world setting.
The analysis of omitted books relies heavily on rhetorical questions, disrupting argumentative flow. Replace some questions with clear claims supported by translator notes or scholarly sources.
The link to the prompt is implied but not explicit. State directly how translator decisions reflect a prioritization of what knowledge is âusefulâ versus âunworthy.â
Could the student clarify whose perspective is taken as normative when judging the usefulness of removed texts? This deepens engagement with TOK perspective.
Good reflection on potential loss of apocryphal knowledge. To strengthen, name one omitted book and outline its contents to anchor your argument in concrete evidence.
Incwala festival is an excellent real-world object. To clarify context, state why it was chosen to illustrate how cultural gatekeepers decide which ritual knowledge is useful.
The photograph engages visually but lacks a caption. Include the year taken and source to reinforce the objectâs real-world authenticity.
Insightful link between secrecy and value of knowledge. To avoid repetition, explore another TOK concept such as knowledge preservation versus accessibility.
General statements about ritual secrecy would benefit from a specific example of a ritual element that is withheld, making your analysis more concrete.
The commentary could incorporate direct TOK terminology (e.g., personal vs. shared knowledge) to clarify how cultural authorities shape usefulness.
The trust in withheld knowledge is well noted. Introduce a counterexample where secrecy led to loss of trust to show a balanced TOK perspective.
The syllabus table is thorough but purely descriptive. Analyze how the selection of topics reflects decisions about which mathematical knowledge is deemed most useful.
Object 3 label is clear; enhance precision by naming the syllabus fully (e.g., CIE Further Mathematics 9231, 2020â22) to anchor real-world context.
Personal comparison of IB and A-level is engaging. To strengthen, link curricular choices to how gatekeepers define usefulness and cite an external curriculum review.
Summarize key syllabus topics rather than listing all, then connect each summary point to why that topic is viewed as âusefulâ by curriculum designers.
Incorporate at least one academic source on curriculum design to substantiate claims about trends influencing syllabus content.
The unanswered questions about syllabus intent are engaging but too numerous. Select one or two central questions to develop a deeper analysis.
Conclusion restates a universal claim but does not synthesize key insights from each object. Summarize how each object addressed the prompt to demonstrate cohesion.
The final paragraph would be stronger if it explicitly answered the prompt and referenced all three objects rather than a general statement on knowledge purpose.
The works cited section shows effort but lacks a consistent citation style. Adopt MLA or APA conventions fully, including authors and publication details.
Several entries omit authors and access dates. Provide full bibliographic information, including title, author/editor, publisher, date, and retrieval details.
Long redirect URLs can become invalid. Use stable links or DOIs and truncate URLs in citations to maintain accessibility.
Consider adding TOK-specific scholarly sources or the IB guide to support conceptual claims about knowledge usefulness.
The text sometimes blurs cultural and personal knowledge. Clarify these distinctions by explicitly naming which type you reference when discussing secrecy.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
